info.name: Registered Funds
info.description:
Complete flow for creating a Registered Fund subscription using the Transaction API (V2).

This collection implements the following workflow:
1. Investor Onboarding
2. Select Registered Fund by symbol
3. Create Subscription Transaction
4. Fetch Preview Documents
5. Complete Investor Transaction Actions (DocumentAcknowledge, TextAcknowledge, DocumentSign, Questionnaire)
6. Complete Advisor Transaction Actions
7. Fetch Signed Documents

**Important Notes:**
- All transaction actions (DocumentAcknowledge, TextAcknowledge, DocumentSign, Questionnaire) are completed via the /transaction-action/{id}/complete endpoint
- For Questionnaire actions, the collection automatically fetches questionnaire details, generates random answers for all question types, and submits them
- The system automatically signs documents when all pending actions for a responsible party are completed

**Supported Question Types:**
- Integer, Float, Percentage
- MultipleChoiceSingle, MultipleChoiceMultiple
- Boolean, Date, Text, Email, Scale

**Setup:**
1. Import the environment file: Registered Fund.postman_environment.json
2. Set `baseUrl` and `apiKey` in the environment
3. Run manually (click Send on each request) OR use Collection Runner (Ctrl/Cmd+R)

request.description: Fetch all financial institutions and select the one.

exec: throw new Error('financialInstitutionId is required. Run "Get All Financial Institutions" first.');
// Generate random SSN (XXX-XX-XXXX format - IRS standard)
// Avoids invalid patterns: area 000/666/900-999, group 00, serial 0000
// Generate random date of birth (between 25-65 years old)

body.raw:
{
	"investorReferenceId": "{{investorReferenceId}}",
	"financialInstitutionId": "{{financialInstitutionId}}",
	"type": "IndividualInvestor",
	"pii": {
		"individualInvestor": {
			"firstName": "John",
			"lastName": "Doe",
			"isUSBased": true,
			"dateOfBirth": "{{randomDOB}}",
			"street1": "123 Market Street",
			"street2": "Apt 456",
			"city": "San Francisco",
			"state": "CA",
			"zipCode": "94103",
			"countryCode": "US",
			"phoneCountryCode": "1",
			"phoneNumber": "{{randomPhone}}",
			"mailingStreet1": "123 Market Street",

			"exemptPayeeCode": "1",
			"isSubscriptionAdvisorOrERA": true,
			"email": "{{randomEmail}}",
			"taxId": "{{randomSSN}}",
			"citizenship": "US",
			"qualifiedStatus": "QUALIFIED_CLIENT"
		}
	}
}

item.description: Complete all transaction actions where responsibleParty is Investor. All action types (DocumentAcknowledge, TextAcknowledge, DocumentSign, Questionnaire) use the same /complete endpoint. For Questionnaire actions, the collection fetches questionnaire details from the dataId, generates random answers for all question types, submits them, and completes the action. Documents are signed automatically when all investor actions are completed.

item.name: 7. Fetch Signed Documents
7.1 Get Signed Documents by Transaction ID

exec: // Store the first document for download

request.description: Fetch all signed documents for the transaction using the V2 Document API. Returns a list of documents with their download URLs.

exec: console.log('Skipping: No document download URL available');
// Get the redirect location (S3 presigned URL)
console.log('Note: Use this URL to download the actual PDF document.');
console.log('The presigned URL is valid for 15 minutes (900 seconds).');
console.error('No redirect location found in response');

request.description:
Download the first signed document. This endpoint returns a 302 redirect to an S3 presigned URL where the actual PDF can be downloaded. The presigned URL is valid for 15 minutes.

Note: If multiple documents exist, only the first one is downloaded. If you need to download additional documents, manually run this request again after updating the 'firstDocumentDownloadUrl' variable with another document's URL from the previous step.

Important: Postman does not store documents larger than 300KB in the response body.
Verify final transaction status after completing all transaction actions.

item.description: Fetch signed documents for the transaction and optionally download them. The V2 Document API returns download URLs that redirect to S3 presigned URLs valid for 15 minutes.

exec: // Auto-clear collection variables when starting a Collection Runner run
// This prevents stale data from previous runs while allowing manual execution
// Only run at the VERY FIRST request (1.1 Get All Financial Institutions)
const isFirstRequest = pm.info.requestName === '1.1 Get All Financial Institutions';
console.log('Collection Runner detected - Clearing all variables');
// Clear all collection variables used in this workflow
// Mark that we've initialized this run
// Clear the run initialization flag at the end of the collection
// This ensures the next Collection Runner run will clear variables again
const isLastRequest = pm.info.requestName === '6.3 Get Final Transaction Status';
console.log('Collection run complete - Ready for next run');
